book_title: Market Wizards: Interviews with Top Traders
author: Jack D. Schwager

traders.name: Michael Marcus
key_rules: Confluence: fundamentals + technicals + market tone must align
Max 5% risk per trade
Ride winners aggressively, cut losers fast
If position makes you uncomfortable, cut size in half

traders.name: Bruce Kovner
key_rules: Trade breakouts from tight congestion zones
Place stops beyond technical barriers (not arbitrary amounts)
Risk 1-2% per trade
Always know worst-case scenario before entering

traders.name: Richard Dennis
key_rules: Turtle Trading: buy 20-day high breakout, sell 10-day low breakdown
Position sizing based on volatility (ATR/N-factor)
Works across all liquid markets (futures, forex, commodities)
53% win rate is profitable with proper R:R
Never risk more than 2% per position

traders.name: Paul Tudor Jones
key_rules: Range expansion: enter when volatility expands from compression
Elliott Wave for macro structure (not precise timing)
Time stops: if trade doesn't work in expected time, exit
Never risk more than 2% in a single session
Reduce size during losing streaks
Strongest position at trend reversals, not mid-trend

traders.name: Gary Bielfeldt
key_rules: Fundamentals for direction, technicals for timing
Poker analogy: fold bad hands, bet big on strong hands
Add to winners systematically
Always have a mechanical backup system

traders.name: Ed Seykota
key_rules: Computerized trend-following with exponential moving averages
Priorities: (1) long-term trend (2) chart pattern (3) entry point
Risk <5% per trade, typically 0.5-2%
Cut losses short, ride winners, keep bets small, follow rules without question
Turned $5,000 into $15,000,000+ over 12 years

traders.name: Larry Hite (Mint Investment)
key_rules: Principle 1: Never risk more than 1% of capital per trade
Principle 2: Always follow trends (never counter-trend)
Principle 3: Extreme diversification across 60+ markets and multiple systems
Principle 4: Track volatility with green/yellow/red regime signals
30%+ annual returns, worst 12-month period <1% loss
Don't try to be right, try to make money

traders.name: Michael Steinhardt
key_rules: Variant perception: be contrarian but only at the right time
Long/short flexibility across stocks, bonds, forex
High conviction positions when thesis is strong
No mechanical rules - pure judgment

traders.name: William O'Neil
key_rules: CANSLIM system:
C = Current quarterly earnings up 20-50%+
A = Annual earnings growth 24%+
N = New product/management/price high
S = Shares outstanding <25 million
L = Leader with Relative Strength >80
I = Institutional sponsorship increasing
M = Market direction (only buy in uptrends)
7% hard stop loss on all positions
Buy stocks making new highs on heavy volume

traders.name: David Ryan
key_rules: Refined CANSLIM: RS>90, EPS rank>80, shares<30M, P/E 1-2x S&P
50% win rate but big winners pay for all losers
Keep a trader's diary (review all trades)
Cut 50% if stock reenters its base after breakout

traders.name: Marty Schwartz
key_rules: Moving averages are the key indicator (especially 10-day EMA)
Oscillators for overbought/oversold confirmation
Reduce size after big wins AND big losses
Know your uncle point (max loss before exiting)
T-bills and T-bonds must confirm equity direction
Turned $40K into $20M, never had >3% drawdown

traders.name: Jim Rogers
key_rules: Buy extreme value, wait for catalyst
Sell hysteria/euphoria
Trade very rarely (months between trades)
Never follow conventional wisdom

traders.name: Mark Weinstein
key_rules: Multiple technicals must align: Elliott, Gann, Fibonacci, cycles, sentiment, MAs, oscillators
Only trade when everything lines up perfectly
~99% win rate claimed
Cheetah analogy: wait patiently for perfect setup

traders.name: Brian Gelber
key_rules: Read the tape, listen to 25+ traders for sentiment
Go with who is currently hot (momentum of traders)
Reactive not opinionated
Never add to losing positions
Wipe the slate clean during streaks

traders.name: Tom Baldwin
key_rules: Pit scalping: average 4-tick profit
Charts for perspective only, not timing
Don't think about money during trading
90% winning days
Patience: pick your spot to exit losers

traders.name: Tony Saliba
key_rules: Butterfly positions in front-month (time decay income)
Explosion positions in back-month (volatility bets)
Scale in and out gradually
Always know worst-case scenario at all times
70 consecutive months >$100K profit

traders.name: Dr. Van K. Tharp
key_rules: 5 beliefs of successful traders:
1. Money is not important
2. It's okay to lose in the market
3. Trading is a game
4. Mental rehearsal before trading
5. You've won the game before you start
Three factors: beliefs, mental states, strategies

strategies.name: Turtle Breakout System
strategies.entry_logic: Buy when price closes above 20-day high. Sell short when price closes below 20-day low.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit longs at 10-day low. Exit shorts at 10-day high. ATR-based trailing stop.
strategies.take_profit: None - ride the trend, exit on trailing stop or channel exit
filters: Only trade in direction of longer-term trend (optional)
Skip if recent breakout failed within 5 bars
strategies.notes: One of the most well-documented systematic strategies in history. Dennis proved markets are teachable. Works best in trending markets, suffers in chop.

strategies.name: Seykota EMA Trend System
strategies.entry_logic: Enter long when short EMA crosses above long EMA and price is above both. Enter short when short EMA crosses below long EMA and price is below both.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when EMA cross reverses. Trailing stop at 3x ATR from highest close.
strategies.position_sizing: Risk 0.5-2% per trade based on conviction and correlation
strategies.stop_loss: 3 * ATR from entry or below longer EMA
filters: Long-term trend must confirm (price above 200-day MA)
Chart pattern should support (consolidation breakout preferred)
strategies.notes: Seykota's actual system parameters are proprietary but the framework is well-documented. $5K to $15M+ over 12 years. Prioritizes: (1) long-term trend (2) chart pattern (3) entry point.

strategies.name: Hite/Mint Diversified Trend System
strategies.entry_logic: Follow price trends using moving average crossovers. Enter in direction of dominant trend across multiple timeframes.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit on trend reversal signal. Volatility regime shift (green→yellow→red) reduces position size.
strategies.position_sizing: Never risk more than 1% per trade. Diversify across 60+ markets.
filters: Volatility regime filter: green (normal), yellow (elevated), red (extreme)
Correlation filter: reduce if too many correlated positions
strategies.notes: Mint Investment Management: 30%+ annual, worst 12-month <1% loss. Emphasis on diversification and risk management over signal quality.

strategies.name: CANSLIM Growth Stock System
strategies.entry_logic: Buy when all CANSLIM criteria met: Current earnings up 20%+, Annual growth 24%+, New product/high, Small float <25M shares, Leader RS>80, Institutional sponsorship, Market in uptrend. Buy on breakout from proper base on heavy volume.
strategies.exit_logic: 7% hard stop loss. Sell at 20-25% profit if reached quickly. Hold for bigger gains if stock moves steadily.
strategies.position_sizing: Concentrate in 4-8 best ideas. Full position on strongest setups.
strategies.take_profit: 20-25% quick profit, or ride with trailing stop in strong trends
filters: Market must be in confirmed uptrend (M factor)
Relative Strength rank >80 (Ryan uses >90)
Must be breaking out of proper base pattern
Volume must be 50%+ above average on breakout day
strategies.notes: O'Neil built IBD/Investors Business Daily around this system. Ryan won US Investing Championship 3 years in a row using refined version. Equity-only strategy.

strategies.name: Schwartz Moving Average System
strategies.entry_logic: Buy when price pulls back to 10-day EMA in uptrend (price above 10 EMA which is above 20 EMA). Sell when price rallies to 10-day EMA in downtrend. Confirm with oscillator reaching oversold/overbought.
strategies.exit_logic: Exit when price closes decisively below 10 EMA (longs) or above 10 EMA (shorts). Scale out on strength.
strategies.position_sizing: Reduce size after big wins or big losses. Standard 1-2% risk.
strategies.stop_loss: Below swing low for longs, above swing high for shorts
filters: T-bills and T-bonds must confirm direction
Know your uncle point before entering
Reduce exposure in choppy markets
strategies.notes: Schwartz: $40K to $20M, never had >3% monthly drawdown. Moving averages are his single most important indicator. Very similar to SBRS MA cross concept.

strategies.name: Kovner Congestion Breakout
strategies.entry_logic: Identify tight congestion zones (low volatility compression). Enter on first decisive close outside the congestion range. Direction bias from fundamental analysis.
strategies.exit_logic: Stop beyond the opposite side of congestion zone. Trail stop as trend develops.
strategies.position_sizing: Risk 1-2% per trade. Position size = risk / distance_to_stop.
strategies.stop_loss: Beyond technical barrier on opposite side of congestion
strategies.take_profit: Measured move: width of congestion projected from breakout point
filters: Congestion must be genuine tight range (not wide choppy range)
Fundamental backdrop should support direction
Volume should increase on breakout
strategies.notes: Kovner averaged 87% annual returns. Congestion breakout is one of few mechanical concepts he described. Similar to SBRS breakout concept but without retest requirement.

strategies.name: Tudor Jones Range Expansion
strategies.entry_logic: Enter when daily range expands significantly (>1.5x 10-day average range) from a compression zone. Direction confirmed by close position within the range (close near high = bullish).
strategies.exit_logic: Time stop: if no follow-through in 2-3 days, exit. Trail stop at prior day's low (longs) or high (shorts).
strategies.position_sizing: Never risk more than 2% in a single session. Reduce size during losing streaks.
strategies.stop_loss: Below expansion bar low (longs) or above expansion bar high (shorts)
strategies.take_profit: No fixed target - ride with trailing stop
filters: Prior compression required (5+ days of below-average range)
Best at potential reversal points (Elliott Wave context)
Reduce size if on losing streak
strategies.notes: Jones is famous for calling 1987 crash. Range expansion concept is his most codifiable idea. Rest of his approach is macro-discretionary.

strategies.name: Saliba Butterfly + Explosion
strategies.entry_logic: Butterfly: Sell front-month ATM options, buy wings for protection. Profits from time decay. Explosion: Buy back-month OTM options cheaply, profits from volatility expansion.
strategies.exit_logic: Butterfly: Close at 50% max profit or roll at 21 DTE. Explosion: Close when volatility spikes or at 2-3x cost.
strategies.position_sizing: Scale in gradually. Never full position at once.
strategies.stop_loss: Butterfly: max loss is defined by spread width. Explosion: lose entire premium (sized accordingly).
strategies.take_profit: Butterfly: 50% of max profit. Explosion: 2-3x cost basis.
filters: Butterfly: IV rank should be elevated (>50th percentile)
Explosion: IV rank should be low (<25th percentile)
Always know worst case at all times
strategies.notes: Options-specific strategy. 70 consecutive profitable months. Not directly applicable to SBRS framework but risk management principles overlap.

cross_cutting_themes.consensus: Never risk more than 1-2% per trade. Cut losses fast. This is the single most agreed-upon rule across all traders.
Following established trends is the most reliable edge. Don't fight the trend.
The payoff distribution is skewed: a few big winners pay for many small losers. Don't cut winners short.
When losing, reduce position size. Protect capital for the next opportunity.
Moving average crossovers are the most commonly used systematic signal across these traders.
